stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {s : String}, Γ ⇒ #s → Γ ≠ [#s] → (∃ L B A Δ Λ, Γ = L ++ [B ⧸ A] ++ Δ ++ Λ ∧ Δ ⇒ A ∧ L ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ #s) ∨ (∃ Γ₁ Δ A B Λ, Γ = Γ₁ ++ Δ ++ [A ⧹ B] ++ Λ ∧ Δ ⇒ A ∧ Γ₁ ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ #s)
priority_rationale: This weaker decomposition statement is already subsumed by the verified atomic last-step theorem and adds little new structure.

stmt: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Tp} {A B : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (A ⧹ B) → Δ ⇒ A → Δ ++ Γ ⇒ B
priority_rationale: This is a standard transport lemma in an overexplored pattern and would not change the theory summary.

stmt: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Tp} {A B : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (B ⧸ A) → Δ ⇒ A → Γ ++ Δ ⇒ B
priority_rationale: This is another standard transport lemma in an overexplored pattern and does not advance the current structural frontier.

stmt: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Tp} {A B C : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (A ⧹ B) → Δ ⇒ (B ⧹ C) → Γ ++ Δ ⇒ (A ⧹ C)
priority_rationale: This composition lemma is locally natural but belongs to an overworked family that does not create new canonical structure.

stmt: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Tp} {A B C : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (C ⧸ B) → Δ ⇒ (B ⧸ A) → Γ ++ Δ ⇒ (C ⧸ A)
priority_rationale: This mirror composition lemma is similarly local and overexplored relative to the present exact-classification agenda.

stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (∀ x ∈ Γ, is_atom x) → Γ ⇒ A → is_atom A ∧ Γ = [A]
priority_rationale: This statement is already refuted by a verified counterexample, so it cannot contribute to the live frontier.

stmt: ∀ {A : Tp} {s : String}, [A] ⇒ Tp.atom s ↔ A = Tp.atom s
summary_delta: The counterexample shows singleton rigidity fails in general, so the first sharp salvage is to ask whether it becomes exact again at atomic conclusions. This is a genuine boundary statement, not a cosmetic restriction, and it would connect the new failure directly to the existing atomic-generation phenomenon.
priority_rationale: This statement is already verified, so solving it now would not move the frontier.

stmt: ∀ s : String, ∃ A : Tp, ¬ is_atom A ∧ [Tp.atom s] ⇒ A
summary_delta: This turns the single counterexample into a uniform separation theorem: even the smallest purely atomic context can derive a compound type. That is the sharp converse-failure lesson exposed by the refutation, and it rules out any attempt to recover conclusion-atomicity without adding a real extra hypothesis.
priority_rationale: A uniform negative strengthening of the known counterexample is cleaner than a single instance, but current pressure favors positive exact criteria over more converse-failure results.

summary_delta: The verified counterexample kills one-way singleton rigidity, but it does not yet say whether equality is still exactly the symmetric boundary of singleton interderivability. Resolving this would distinguish a mere one-sided expansion phenomenon from a genuine collapse of the singleton derivability preorder, which is a sharper and more reusable structural lesson.
priority_rationale: This is a sharp boundary question for singleton interderivability and could distinguish one-way expansion from genuine collapse, though it does not yet build the recursive focused interface.

stmt: Every derivation [A] ⇒ B admits a finite focused singleton decomposition tree obtained by iterating the outermost slash classification on the succedent until the distinguished singleton branch reaches A, with each step recording the corresponding side sequent generated by that slash.
summary_delta: This is the missing bridge from the newly solved one-step singleton split to a global proof-search object. It would explain exactly how singleton derivations grow, make the surviving structure behind failed singleton rigidity explicit, and provide a reusable normal-form interface for later fragment and grammar-facing classifications.
priority_rationale: This is the missing global recursive object on the singleton side and would substantially rewrite the summary by turning the one-step classification into a canonical decomposition theory.

stmt: ∀ {A B : Tp}, [A] ⇒ B → tp_degree A ≤ tp_degree B, and if A ≠ B then tp_degree A < tp_degree B.
summary_delta: This would turn the singleton classification into a sharp separation invariant rather than just an existence description. A strict degree boundary would rule out nontrivial cycles in singleton derivability, strengthen the surviving rigidity boundary after the known counterexample, and supply a reusable obstruction for later normalization and fragment arguments.
priority_rationale: A degree monotonicity invariant would be useful obstruction machinery, but it is a secondary singleton refinement rather than a frontier-defining structural bridge.
